beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and have coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Jackson always starts the day with a swim, but on Sundays the routine is more flexible; still wakes early out of habit and preps for the day with coffee and a quick shower.

beats.activity: Early morning swim at Northgate Pool
beats.reasoning: Jackson can't start the day without a swim—this is non-negotiable ritual that clears his head, especially after a low-energy Saturday (40/100) and an emotionally full week.
Short drive back to Northgate from the pool to freshen up and have a proper breakfast on this calm, sunny Sunday.

beats.activity: Breakfast and relax at home with the newspaper
beats.reasoning: After the swim, Jackson enjoys a leisurely Sunday breakfast at home; he's recovering energy from yesterday's Pride outing and the week's emotional counseling work.
Jackson regularly visits QFC for groceries and yarn; the sunny weather makes this a perfect time for a relaxed Sunday errand near home.
Brief return to Northgate home to put away groceries and settle in before heading out for the afternoon activity.
Jackson loves outdoor recreation and the lake on calm sunny days; the heavy boat traffic on Lake Union today makes it an attractive time to visit Gas Works Park, which overlooks the water.

beats.activity: Afternoon at Gas Works Park: walk, relax, watch boats on Lake Union
beats.reasoning: Spontaneous, mood-driven Seattleite takes advantage of the perfect 72F sunny day and heavy recreational boat traffic to spend a long, restorative afternoon at a beloved park; this balances the low energy from yesterday.

beats.activity: Drive home from Gas Works Park
beats.reasoning: Return to Northgate; the afternoon is winding down and Jackson prefers to be home before evening.

beats.activity: Light lunch, then knitting and podcast time on the porch
beats.reasoning: Jackson values quiet time with needlework and audio storytelling; spending the late afternoon at home with knitting and a podcast is his preferred way to ease into evening after outdoor time.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home
beats.reasoning: Jackson cooks a simple dinner on Sunday evening; after a week of school counseling and yesterday's social outing, a quiet meal at home fits his mood and energy level.

beats.activity: Evening knitting, podcast, and wind-down at home
beats.reasoning: Jackson spends the evening doing what grounds him: knitting with a podcast, allowing the day to fade gently as he prepares for the school week ahead.
Seven and a half hours of sleep to restore energy before the school week; Jackson goes to bed early on Sunday to be ready for counseling sessions on Monday.

diary:
Woke early and did my usual swim at Northgate Pool—the water was perfect this morning, calm and cool. There's something about those early laps that sets the whole day right. After breakfast with the paper, I hit QFC for groceries and grabbed some lovely yarn in this soft sage green. Been meaning to start a new project for weeks.

Drove out to Gas Works Park around mid-morning. The lake was absolutely packed with boats and kayakers, all these bright colors dotting the water. I walked the park for a couple hours, just breathing it all in. Found a quiet spot on the grass and watched the city hum below me. Later at home, settled onto the porch with my knitting and a podcast—one of those true crime ones that's weirdly soothing. Dinner was simple. Spent the evening knitting, letting my hands do the work while my mind drifted. Felt peaceful.
thought_process: Today felt like a gift, honestly. Sunday mornings always pull me toward the pool—it's my anchor before everything else. The grocery run was practical, but picking out that yarn reminded me I need more creative time, more things just for me. Gas Works called to me because of the weather, and seeing all those boats on Lake Union felt alive and energizing without being overwhelming. I've been leaning into solo activities lately—knitting, podcasts, long walks—and I think that's been good for my mindset. At 59, I'm learning to value quiet contentment over obligation. The day's calm rhythm matched my mood perfectly, and I didn't fight it.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
